title: Fraunhofer IPMS Unveils Q-Dice Quantum Random Number Generator for Enterprise Security - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Fraunhofer IPMS unveiled Q-Dice, a quantum random number generator delivering true random numbers above 4.1 Gbit/s by measuring quantum vacuum fluctuations, fully certified (NIST SP 800-22, BSI AIS 20/31, PTG 3, EAL 3) and commercialized both as a rack appliance and as cloud Entropy-as-a-Service for government, military, financial and telecom infrastructure.

insights.claim: Fraunhofer IPMS's Q-Dice QRNG delivers true random numbers at data rates exceeding 4.1 Gbit/s by physically measuring quantum vacuum fluctuations, replacing deterministic software algorithms that can introduce structural predictability vulnerabilities.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A concrete high-throughput QRNG benchmark (4.1 Gbit/s) for security infrastructure; quantum entropy hardware is reaching enterprise data rates today, independent of fault-tolerant quantum computing.
insights.quote: engineered to deliver true random numbers at data rates exceeding 4.1 Gbit/s.

insights.claim: Q-Dice is positioned to future-proof communication networks against both classical and quantum computing threats by providing an unbiased cryptographic entropy foundation.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: QRNG hardware is being marketed as part of the post-quantum security stack; a near-term commercial quantum-security category distinct from quantum computers.
insights.quote: The architecture provides an unbiased cryptographic foundation designed to future-proof communication networks against both classical and quantum computing threats.

insights.claim: Q-Dice's random output is validated under NIST SP 800-22 and German BSI AIS 20/31, and achieved PTG 3 physical-true-RNG classification and EAL 3, certifying readiness for government, military, financial and telecom infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Full regulatory certification removes a key procurement barrier for high-assurance buyers; a differentiator for selling into regulated/sovereign markets.
insights.quote: It has achieved a physical security classification of PTG 3 (Physical True Random Number Generator) and an Evaluation Assurance Level of EAL 3, certifying its readiness for deployment within critical government, military, financial, and telecommunication infrastructure.
insights.locator: Regulatory Compliances and Dual Deployment Models

insights.claim: Fraunhofer IPMS commercializes Q-Dice via two models: a 19-inch rack-mountable hardware appliance for corporate data centers, and an API-driven cloud Entropy-as-a-Service that streams quantum random numbers over the web without on-premises hardware.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The Entropy-as-a-Service model lowers adoption friction for prototyping and could seed a recurring-revenue quantum-security cloud category.
insights.quote: Online QRNG on Demand (Entropy-as-a-Service): A cloud-linked, API-driven software service that allows researchers, developers, and systems integrators to purchase and securely stream quantum-generated true random numbers over the web
insights.locator: Fraunhofer IPMS is commercializing the technology through two distinct operational delivery methods

insights.claim: Q-Dice's noise engine underpins EU state-funded secure-identity initiatives including the CBQD chip-based quantum random device project and the Quant-ID consortium, focused on high-speed post-quantum cryptography validation keys.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals EU-backed funding flowing into quantum-secure identity and PQC infrastructure; track CBQD/Quant-ID for European sovereign-security demand.
insights.quote: including the integrated CBQD (Chip-based quantum random device) project and the Quant-ID (Quantum Secure Identities for a Digital Future) consortium.
